1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2026 The AlgebraicJacobian Project. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license.

5	import Mathlib
6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.CechTermAcyclic
7	
8	/-!
9	# Pullback preserves quasi-coherence (Stacks 01BG, general morphism)
10	
11	For an arbitrary morphism of schemes `g : Y ⟶ X` and a quasi-coherent
12	`F : X.Modules`, the module pullback `g^* F = (Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj F`
13	is quasi-coherent (`pullback_isQuasicoherent_hom`).  This generalizes the
14	open-immersion case `isQuasicoherent_pullback_opens` (`CechTermAcyclic.lean`);
15	the previously-documented obstruction — `SheafOfModules.Presentation.map` only
16	transports a *global* presentation while quasi-coherence is *local* — is resolved
17	by localizing the pullback itself: over each member `U` of the quasi-coherence
18	cover of `X`, the restriction of `g^* F` to the preimage `W = g⁻¹(U)` is the
19	pullback of `F|_U` along the restricted morphism `g.resLE U W : W ⟶ U`
20	(the pullback pseudofunctor square `resLE_comp_ι`), and the *global* presentation
21	of `F|_U` transports along that restricted pullback.
22	
23	Two supporting facts are proved on the way:
24	
25	* `opensMap_final`: for any continuous map `φ`, the preimage functor
26	  `Opens.map φ` on opens is `Final` — the costructured comma poset
27	  `{U // d ≤ φ⁻¹(U)}` is nonempty (`⊤`) and `⊔`-directed, hence filtered.
28	* `pullbackObjUnitToUnit_isIso_hom`: consequently Mathlib's unit comparison
29	  `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` is an isomorphism for the module pullback along *any*
30	  scheme morphism, i.e. `g^* 𝒪_X ≅ 𝒪_Y` (`pullbackUnitIso`).
31	
32	These feed the flat-base-change RHS tilde leaf
33	`pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChanged_pushforward_iso_tilde`
34	(`CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean`), which needs quasi-coherence of the
35	base-changed module `g'^* F` to apply the altitude-2 bridge
36	`pushPullObj_pushforward_iso_tilde` on the base-changed side.
37	-/
38	
39	universe u
40	
41	set_option maxSynthPendingDepth 3
42	
43	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace
44	
45	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
46	
47	open Scheme.Modules
48	
49	/-! ## Finality of the opens-preimage functor -/
50	
51	/-- The costructured comma category `StructuredArrow d (Opens.map φ)` — the poset of opens
52	`U` of the target with `d ≤ φ⁻¹(U)` — is filtered: it is nonempty (`U = ⊤`) and closed
53	under binary joins (`φ⁻¹` is monotone and preserves `⊔`), and it is thin, so parallel
54	pairs are automatically equalized. -/
55	instance opensMapStructuredArrow_isFiltered {T T' : TopCat.{u}} (φ : T ⟶ T')
56	    (d : Opens T) : IsFiltered (StructuredArrow d (Opens.map φ)) := by
57	  haveI hne : Nonempty (StructuredArrow d (Opens.map φ)) :=
58	    ⟨StructuredArrow.mk (Y := ⊤) (homOfLE (fun x _ => trivial))⟩
59	  haveI hfe : IsFilteredOrEmpty (StructuredArrow d (Opens.map φ)) := by
60	    constructor
61	    · intro j₁ j₂
62	      refine ⟨StructuredArrow.mk (Y := j₁.right ⊔ j₂.right)
63	          (j₁.hom ≫ (Opens.map φ).map (homOfLE le_sup_left)),
64	        StructuredArrow.homMk (homOfLE le_sup_left) rfl,
65	        StructuredArrow.homMk (homOfLE le_sup_right) (Subsingleton.elim _ _), trivial⟩
66	    · intro j₁ j₂ f g
67	      exact ⟨j₂, 𝟙 j₂, by apply StructuredArrow.hom_ext; exact Subsingleton.elim _ _⟩
68	  constructor
69	
70	/-- **The opens-preimage functor of any continuous map is final.**  Consequently global
71	sections of a pushforward along any scheme morphism compare correctly, and the module
72	pullback of the structure sheaf is the structure sheaf (`pullbackObjUnitToUnit` is an
73	isomorphism, Mathlib's `[F.Final]` instance in `PullbackFree.lean`). -/
74	instance opensMap_final {T T' : TopCat.{u}} (φ : T ⟶ T') : (Opens.map φ).Final :=
75	  Functor.final_of_isFiltered_structuredArrow _
76	
77	set_option maxHeartbeats 1000000 in
78	set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 400000 in
79	/-- **`g^* 𝒪_X ≅ 𝒪_Y` for an arbitrary scheme morphism**: the unit comparison of the
80	module pullback along any scheme morphism is an isomorphism (the underlying site functor
81	`Opens.map g.base` is final by `opensMap_final`). -/
82	instance pullbackObjUnitToUnit_isIso_hom {Y X : Scheme.{u}} (g : Y ⟶ X) :
83	    IsIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit g.toRingCatSheafHom) :=
84	  inferInstance
85	
86	/-- The structure-sheaf unit iso `g^* 𝒪_X ≅ 𝒪_Y` for an arbitrary scheme morphism,
87	packaged for `Presentation.map`. -/
88	noncomputable def pullbackUnitIso {Y X : Scheme.{u}} (g : Y ⟶ X) :
89	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) ≅
90	      SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf :=
91	  @asIso _ _ _ _ _ (pullbackObjUnitToUnit_isIso_hom g)
92	
93	/-! ## The per-slice presentation of a pullback -/
94	
95	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
96	set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 1000000 in
97	set_option maxHeartbeats 2000000 in
98	/-- **The per-slice presentation of a pullback**: a presentation of `F.over A` induces a
99	presentation of the slice `(g^* F).over (g ⁻¹ᵁ A)`, for an arbitrary scheme morphism
100	`g : Y ⟶ X`.
101	
102	Route: let `W := g ⁻¹ᵁ A` and `gA := g.resLE A W : W ⟶ A` the restricted morphism.  The
103	X-side bridge (`presentationRestrictOfOver` at `W := A`) presents the full restriction
104	`F.restrict A.ι`; the restricted pullback `(pullback gA)` transports it
105	(`Presentation.map`, unit iso by `pullbackUnitIso`); the pullback pseudofunctor square
106	`gA ≫ A.ι = W.ι ≫ g` (`resLE_comp_ι`) identifies `(pullback gA).obj (F.restrict A.ι)`
107	with `(g^* F).restrict W.ι`; the V-side bridge engine (`modulesOverOpensEquivalence`
108	over `Y`) carries the presentation back to the over-slice. -/
109	noncomputable def presentationPullbackSliceOfOver {Y X : Scheme.{u}} (g : Y ⟶ X)
110	    (F : X.Modules) (A : X.Opens) (P : (F.over A).Presentation) :
111	    (((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj F).over (g ⁻¹ᵁ A)).Presentation := by
112	  set W : Y.Opens := g ⁻¹ᵁ A with hW
113	  set G : Y.Modules := (Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj F with hG
114	  -- X-side: present the full restriction to `A`.
115	  letI P1 : (F.restrict A.ι).Presentation := presentationRestrictOfOver A F A P le_rfl
116	  -- The restricted morphism `W ⟶ A`.
117	  letI gA : W.toScheme ⟶ A.toScheme := g.resLE A W le_rfl
118	  -- Transport the presentation along the restricted pullback (colimit-preserving; unit ↦ unit).
119	  haveI hpc : Limits.PreservesColimitsOfSize.{u, u, u, u, u + 1, u + 1}
120	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback gA) := inferInstance
121	  letI P2 : ((Scheme.Modules.pullback gA).obj (F.restrict A.ι)).Presentation :=
122	    @SheafOfModules.Presentation.map _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ P1
123	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback gA) hpc (pullbackUnitIso gA).symm
124	  -- Identify with the restriction of the global pullback via the pseudofunctor square.
125	  have heq : gA ≫ A.ι = W.ι ≫ g := Scheme.Hom.resLE_comp_ι g le_rfl
126	  letI e : (Scheme.Modules.pullback gA).obj (F.restrict A.ι) ≅ G.restrict W.ι :=
127	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback gA).mapIso
128	        ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback A.ι).app F) ≪≫
129	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp gA A.ι).app F ≪≫
130	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr heq).app F ≪≫
131	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp W.ι g).app F).symm ≪≫
132	      ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback W.ι).app G).symm
133	  letI P5 : (G.restrict W.ι).Presentation :=
134	    SheafOfModules.Presentation.ofIsIso.{u, u, u} e.hom P2
135	  -- V-side: carry the restriction presentation back to the over-slice through the engine.
136	  letI eV := modulesOverOpensEquivalence (X := Y) W
137	  letI P6 : (eV.inverse.obj (G.over W)).Presentation :=
138	    SheafOfModules.Presentation.ofIsIso.{u, u, u}
139	      (overOpensIsoRestrict W G).symm.hom P5
140	  letI ηV : eV.functor.obj (SheafOfModules.unit W.toScheme.ringCatSheaf) ≅
141	      SheafOfModules.unit (Sheaf.over Y.ringCatSheaf W) :=
142	    overOpensFunctorUnitIso (X := Y) W
143	  letI P7 : (eV.functor.obj (eV.inverse.obj (G.over W))).Presentation :=
144	    P6.map eV.functor ηV.symm
145	  exact SheafOfModules.Presentation.ofIsIso.{u, u, u}
146	    (eV.counitIso.app (G.over W)).hom P7
147	
148	/-! ## Pullback preserves quasi-coherence -/
149	
150	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
151	set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 1000000 in
152	set_option maxHeartbeats 2000000 in
153	/-- **Pullback preserves quasi-coherence** (Stacks 01BG, general-morphism case).  For an
154	arbitrary morphism of schemes `g : Y ⟶ X` and a quasi-coherent `F : X.Modules`, the module
155	pullback `g^* F` is quasi-coherent.
156	
157	Quasi-coherence is local (`SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent.of_coversTop`): the preimages
158	under `g` of the quasi-coherence cover of `X` cover `Y`, and over each preimage the
159	per-slice obligation is `presentationPullbackSliceOfOver`. -/
160	theorem pullback_isQuasicoherent_hom {Y X : Scheme.{u}} (g : Y ⟶ X)
161	    (F : X.Modules) (hF : F.IsQuasicoherent) :
162	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj F).IsQuasicoherent := by
163	  obtain ⟨⟨q⟩⟩ := hF
164	  -- The preimages of the quasi-coherence cover of `X` cover `Y`.
165	  have hcov : (Opens.grothendieckTopology Y).CoversTop
166	      (fun i => g ⁻¹ᵁ q.X i) := by
167	    intro W x hx
168	    obtain ⟨U', fU, hf, hU'⟩ := q.coversTop ⊤ (g.base x) (by trivial)
169	    obtain ⟨i, ⟨gi⟩⟩ := hf
170	    refine ⟨W ⊓ (g ⁻¹ᵁ q.X i), homOfLE inf_le_left, ⟨i, ⟨homOfLE inf_le_right⟩⟩,
171	      ⟨hx, ?_⟩⟩
172	    exact (leOfHom gi) hU'
173	  -- Per-slice presentations through the restricted-pullback bridge.
174	  haveI hslice : ∀ i,
175	      (((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj F).over (g ⁻¹ᵁ q.X i)).IsQuasicoherent := fun i =>
176	    (presentationPullbackSliceOfOver g F (q.X i) (q.presentation i)).isQuasicoherent
177	  exact SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent.of_coversTop
178	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj F) (fun i => g ⁻¹ᵁ q.X i) hcov
179	
180	end AlgebraicGeometry

Base directory for this skill: /AI4M/users/Axel/LeanAlgebraicGeometry-Horizon/.claude/skills/horizon-inbox

The inbox is the durable channel between the human, the Ground agent, the
Horizon agent, and other projects. Act on it through the CLI; never edit inbox
files by hand. Prefer `--json` whenever reading command output for exact ids or
fields.

## Read

- `horizon inbox list` — read the inbox. Use `--json` when you need exact ids,
  labels, scope, author, audience, timestamps, or comments. Use the list filters
  to narrow without losing the overview: `--to horizon`, `--to ground`,
  `--to human`, `--project P`, repeated `--kind K`, repeated `--label L`,
  `--status open`, `--query TEXT`, `--limit N`, and `--comments N`.
- Labels are the release gate. Local inbox items default to `agent-ready`, which
  means they are visible to agents at run boundaries. Keep that default for
  machine-addressed work, and usually keep it for human-addressed notices too:
  it helps later agents see that the human has already been notified and avoids
  duplicate reports. Use `not-ready`, `rejected`, or no labels only when there is
  a concrete reason agents should not see or act on the item yet.
- Audience says who should read the item: empty/general, `horizon`, `ground`,
  `human`, or `project:<name>`. Scope/project says what the item is about.
- Horizon reads general items, items addressed to `horizon`, and items addressed
  to its project. It should ignore other projects' addressed items.
- Kinds: `hint` (normal guidance), `issue` (problem to fix/report),
  `protection` (standing constraint / soft freeze), `info` (a notice for the
  human — something you did or noticed they should know, e.g. a renamed project
  or an important change; purely informational, never affects what runs),
  `memory` (durable agent note or dead end).

## Act

Authorship is automatic: every item and comment you create through the CLI is
attributed to your role (`ground` or `horizon`) from the run environment — you do
**not** pass `--author` (a stray `--author blueprint-reviewer` is demoted to the
`agent` metadata, not used as the author, so the author set stays conventional).
If you are a dispatched **subagent**, add `--agent <your-name>` (e.g. `--agent
blueprint-reviewer`) to record your identity; it is stored in metadata and shown
next to the role in the UI, keeping the author itself a clean role. Items and
comments are also auto-tagged with the run/session provenance (a small chip in the
UI); you do not pass this yourself.

New inbox items use a two-part body:

- First paragraph = concise title. Keep it short enough to scan in one UI row
  (aim for under ~100 characters); do not put the full explanation here.
- Second paragraph and later = non-empty description. Put the evidence, impact,
  next action, and relevant ids/files here. The description is **mandatory** — an
  item that is only a title is rejected.

Write bodies and comments in **Markdown**: the dashboard renders it. Use short
paragraphs and `-` bullet lists instead of one wall-of-text paragraph, `**bold**`
for the key claim, and backticks for ids, lemma names, files, and code
(`` `lem:foo` ``, `` `Foo.lean:42` ``). A long unstructured blob is hard to scan.

Do not create an item whose body is only a title. If you need a `[persistent]` or
`[temporary]` tag, put it at the start of the title paragraph and keep the rest
of that paragraph concise.

Before opening a new item, search the open inbox for the same topic using
`horizon inbox list --status open --query "key words" --json` and, when relevant,
`--project P` / `--to R`. If a live item already covers the same issue, do not
open a duplicate: add a comment with the new evidence, or edit the body if the
main description is stale. If you discover an open item is obsolete, already
solved, or superseded, add a short closing comment explaining why and then run
`horizon inbox complete <id>`.

- `horizon inbox comment <id> --body "..."` — progress note on an item (record
  what you did; not to ask the human for clarification). Do not write your name
  into the comment body; the author is set automatically. Drop a comment at each **key advance**
  while you work an item (a milestone reached, a route ruled out, a blocker hit),
  not only at the end — these comments are the item's visible progress trail.
- **Write comments as mathematics, for a human.** State the key step that
  succeeded in human-readable mathematical language (LaTeX for formulas), naming
  the result and the idea — e.g. "Proved $H^i(X,\mathcal{F})=0$ for $i>\dim X$ by
  reducing to the affine case via Čech cohomology." Mention Lean declaration
  names and file paths only as supporting detail; the substance is the maths, not
  a code changelog. This applies to comments on **roadmap items and tasks** too:
  when a key step lands, record it there in the same mathematical, legible style.
- `horizon inbox complete <id>` — close an item once you have acted on it. Before
  closing, add a short **closing comment**: first the mathematical conclusion (what
  was proved/established, in words + LaTeX), then a line of concise metadata
  (runs, approximate LOC, files/declarations involved). A reader should grasp the
  result and how it was concluded without opening the logs.
- `horizon inbox archive <id>` — soft-delete: keep the item for the record but
  hide it from the dashboard by default. Use for stale/superseded items that are
  not worth a closing comment; prefer `complete` (with a comment) for work you
  actually concluded. Ground is responsible for keeping the inbox tidy this way.
- `horizon inbox add --body "Short title\n\nDescription with details and next action." [--to R] [--project P] [--persistent|--temporary] [--agent <name> if a subagent]`
  — open a new item.
  - `--to` is the recipient: `horizon`, `ground`, `human`, or `project:<name>`.
    Use `--to project:<name>` to message another project (e.g. "I rewrote your
    `foo` declaration more cleanly, you may want it").
  - `--project` says what the item is ABOUT (its subject).
  - `--persistent` / `--temporary` just prepend a `[persistent]` / `[temporary]`
    tag to the body.
- `horizon inbox add --kind memory --to horizon --body "Short memory title\n\nDurable fact, convention, or dead end."` — record a durable
  note / dead end. Memory lives in the inbox, so it is rendered in the "Memory"
  section and a human can prune it like any other item.
- `horizon inbox protect --body "..." [--project P] [--file F] [--declaration D]`
  — add a standing constraint the Horizon agent must respect (the soft freeze),
  e.g. "do not change the signature of `Foo.bar`". These render as a
  "Protected" section; honour them, including semantic ones.

Reading convention: an item tagged `[persistent]` is a standing rule — respect
it every round and never close it. `[temporary]` (or untagged) is one-shot —
`complete` it once consumed. Items in the "Protected" section are never modified.
